Guideline: Use BLoC or Riverpod for State
Description: Choose appropriate state management based on app complexity. BLoC for event-driven Riverpod for simpler reactive
Do: Use BLoC for complex state with events; Riverpod for simpler reactive state; setState only for ephemeral UI state
Dont: Use setState for everything; mix multiple state management solutions

Description: Know when initState didChangeDependencies build didUpdateWidget and dispose are called
Do: Initialize in initState; fetch theme/locale in didChangeDependencies; clean up in dispose; use keys for identity
Dont: Fetch data in build; skip dispose; use late without understanding lifecycle

Description: Declarative routing with type-safe parameters deep linking support and nested navigation
Do: Define routes declaratively; use type-safe path parameters; handle redirects for auth; support deep links
Dont: Use Navigator.push/pop everywhere; hardcode route strings; forget auth guards

Description: Define all colors typography and component themes in one place using ThemeData
Do: Use Theme.of(context) everywhere; define color scheme from seed; support dark mode
Dont: Hardcode colors inline; use different colors for same semantic meaning
Code Bad: Text('Hello', style: TextStyle(color: Color(0xFF333333), fontSize: 24));\nContainer(color: Colors.blue); // Random hardcoded colors

Description: Minimize unnecessary rebuilds using const constructors selective watching and RepaintBoundary
Do: Use const constructors; split widgets; use context.select/ref.select; RepaintBoundary for animations
Dont: Rebuild entire tree on state change; use global notifiers that rebuild everything

Description: Test widgets with WidgetTester and BLoCs with blocTest for reliable UI and state testing
Do: Write widget tests for UI; blocTest for state transitions; mock dependencies; test error states
Dont: Only write unit tests; skip widget tests; test implementation details not behavior
Code Bad: // No widget tests\n// Only unit tests on pure functions\n// No integration tests

Guideline: Use Either Type for Error Propagation
Description: Propagate errors through the type system using Either/Result types instead of exceptions
Do: Return Either<Failure, Success>; pattern match on result; define typed failures per domain
Dont: Throw exceptions for expected errors; catch-all silently; return null for errors

Description: Set up Dio HTTP client with interceptors for auth retry logging and error handling
Do: Add auth interceptor; configure timeout; add retry logic; use base options
Dont: Create Dio instances everywhere; add headers manually per request

Description: Generate immutable data classes with Freezed for type-safe states models and union types
Do: Use @freezed for state classes and models; run build_runner; commit generated files or gitignore them
Dont: Write manual copyWith/equals/hashCode; create mutable state classes

Description: Organize code by feature not by type for better scalability and team collaboration
Do: Group files by feature (auth/ cart/ products/); keep each feature self-contained; share via core/shared
Dont: Group by type (all blocs/ all models/ all screens/); scatter related files across directories
Code Good:
lib/
  features/
    auth/
      data/
      domain/
      presentation/
    products/
      data/
      domain/
      presentation/
  core/
  shared/
Code Bad:
lib/
  blocs/
    auth_bloc.dart
    product_bloc.dart
  models/
    user.dart
    product.dart
  screens/
    login_screen.dart
    product_screen.dart

Description: Set up service locator with auto-registration for clean testable dependency management
Do: Use @injectable for auto-registration; @lazySingleton for services; @module for third-party deps
Dont: Create singletons manually; use static instances; pass dependencies through widget constructors deeply

Description: Support multiple languages with official Flutter localization and intl package
Do: Generate .arb files; use context.l10n; support plurals and parameters; test with different locales
Dont: Hardcode strings; use custom translation solutions; forget RTL support
Code Bad: Text('Welcome, $name!'); // Hardcoded English\nText('${cart.length} items'); // No pluralization

Description: Use Pigeon for type-safe platform channel communication instead of manual method channels
Do: Use Pigeon for code generation; define message types; handle errors on both sides
Dont: Use raw MethodChannel strings; forget to handle PlatformException; serialize manually
Code Bad: static const platform = MethodChannel('com.example/device');\nfinal result = await platform.invokeMethod('getDeviceId');\n// No type safety, string-based, error prone

Description: Properly manage images fonts and other assets with correct resolution variants
Do: Use 2x/3x variants; use SVG for icons; compress images; use flutter_gen for type-safe access
Dont: Use only 1x images; load full-res images for thumbnails; hardcode asset paths
Code Good:
# pubspec.yaml
flutter:
  assets:
    - assets/images/
    - assets/icons/
  fonts:
    - family: Inter
      fonts:
        - asset: assets/fonts/Inter-Regular.ttf
        - asset: assets/fonts/Inter-Bold.ttf
          weight: 700

# With flutter_gen
Image.asset(Assets.images.logo.path);
SvgPicture.asset(Assets.icons.cart.path);
Code Bad: Image.asset('assets/images/logo.png'); // Only 1x, blurry on high-DPI\n// No asset variants, no type safety

Description: Automate testing building and deployment with GitHub Actions or similar CI/CD
Do: Run analyze + test + build on every PR; deploy to Firebase App Distribution for beta; use Fastlane for stores
Dont: Skip CI; manual builds and uploads; no automated testing before merge
Code Good:
# .github/workflows/flutter.yml
name: Flutter CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
        with: { flutter-version: '3.16.0' }
      - run: flutter pub get
      - run: flutter analyze --fatal-warnings
      - run: flutter test --coverage
      - run: flutter build apk --release
Code Bad: # No CI/CD\n# Manual: flutter build apk -> upload to Play Store\n# No tests before release
